channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Do robotic lawnmowers actually work and are they worth the investment?
item.description: Experts say robot lawnmowers are becoming more accessible, but there are some key features to look out for.
item.dc:creator: Amy Sheehan

media:group.media:description: Robot lawnmowers can cost several thousand dollars, depending on the type of technology they use.

item.title: Another four platypuses re-homed into Royal National Park
item.description: There are at least 20 platypuses now living in the NSW Royal National Park's waterways, three years after their historic reintroduction.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Chandler

media:group.media:description: Seventeen adult platypuses have been re-homed into the Royal National Park's Hacking River since the project began in 2023.

item.title: Eleven Australian flotilla activists released from Israeli detention
item.description: Social Services Minister Tanya Plibersek has told ABC News Breakfast that the 11 Australians are now in Istanbul and are being assisted by Australian officials.

media:group.media:description: Activists from the Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla were flown to Türkiye.

item.title: Mother's love leads to cricket team representing Australia in the UK
item.description: A Queensland cricket team, started by a mother who wanted to give her autistic daughter a safe space to play, has just embarked on an international tour.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Ross and Sarah Howells

media:group.media:description: The Nambour USC Strikers are preparing to represent Australia in a grassroots cricket competition.

item.title: Republican revolt over Trump 'weaponisation' fund stalls ICE funding vote
item.description: US Senate Republicans have furious internal disagreements over a proposed compensation fund for Trump allies who claim they have been unfairly targeted by federal agencies.

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump's proposed fund for what he calls victims of government "weaponisation" is facing resistance from Senate Republicans.

item.title: 'Classes of 50 children': Full-day ACT public teacher strike flagged
item.description: Canberra public schools were closed until 10:30am today, with more industrial action expected in June.

media:group.media:description: Teacher Olivia Neilson said ACT public school staff and students deserved better.

item.title: The 600-kilometre trip to the skin specialist
item.description: Regional Queensland is facing a shortage of dermatologists, leaving some patients increasingly desperate.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gamble

media:group.media:description: There is currently no locally based dermatologist in Rockhampton.

item.title: NT Chief Minister dismisses concerns about child protection changes as 'hysteria'
item.description: Northern Territory Chief Minister Lia Finocchiaro has labelled concerns about proposed child protection changes as "hysteria", a day after the prime minister called for "respectful" consultation with outraged Aboriginal groups.
item.dc:creator: Jacqueline Breen, Lara Stimpson, Elsie Lange, and Stewart Brash

media:group.media:description: Lia Finocchiaro dismissed concerns about changes to child protection laws.

item.title: Farmers struggle to source popular cattle feed as dry conditions bite
item.description: A by-product of the cotton harvest, cottonseed boosts protein and fibre in animal diets. But as graziers ramp up their drought preparations, dry conditions are causing a supply slowdown.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Johnson

media:group.media:description: Cottonseed availability has been limited this season, as growers are slower to sell their cotton into the market.

item.title: Queensland premier learned of minister's AFP referral through media
item.description: Questions have been raised about how long Tim Mander waited to tell the Queensland premier about his referral to the AFP.
item.dc:creator: Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: David Crisafulli's office found out about the referral of his Olympics minister to federal police via the media.

item.title: Law allowing flooding on private land 'critical' for environment, expert says
item.description: Controversial new legislation could secure critical water supplies for delicate ecosystems while delivering benefits to the Murray-Darling Basin's agricultural sector, a leading river ecologist says.
item.dc:creator: Melinda Hayter

media:group.media:description: Agriculture and environmental ecosystems exist side by side across the Murray-Darling Basin.

item.title: Global mental health disorder cases double in three decades
item.description: A surge over the last three decades means mental disorders now account for more than 17 per cent of all disability worldwide, according to a report published this week.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Morris-Grant

media:group.media:description: New research says the number of mental health cases worldwide has almost doubled over the past three decades.

item.title: What is 'pink-slime' journalism and has it infiltrated Australian media?
item.description: Experts say a series of AI-generated digital news outlets is the first recorded example of "pink-slime" journalism in Australia, with the website owner claiming the now-deleted outlets were an experiment gone wrong.
item.dc:creator: Madigan Landry

media:group.media:description: The Bunbury Guardian was one of a series of AI-generated news sites that appeared in regional WA earlier this year.

item.title: 'Absolute shock': NASCAR champion Kyle Busch dies aged 41
item.description: Kyle Busch, who won two Cup Series championships and will be a future NASCAR Hall of Famer, has died aged 41.

media:group.media:description: NASCAR champion Kyle Busch has died aged 41 after being admitted to hospital this morning.

item.title: DNA study finds many dingoes may have some domestic dog genetics
item.description: A new study into dingo DNA has found many animals previously considered to be purebred actually have domestic dog genetics.
item.dc:creator: Angus Verley

media:group.media:description: The study found many dingoes previously considered purebred have domestic dog ancestry.

item.title: 'Is that a UFO?' Suspected meteor lights up night sky
item.description: Colourful streaks across the sky have been spotted across eastern Australia as a "fireball" meteor passed through the atmosphere.
item.dc:creator: Miriah Davis, Julia André, and Declan Bowring

media:group.media:description: Footage showing a meteor in the sky over Jugiong last night.

item.title: US-bound plane diverted after passenger from Ebola-hit Congo boards 'in error'
item.description: The US has introduced new restrictions on travellers from countries exposed to an Ebola outbreak in Africa, requiring them to enter through Washington DC.

media:group.media:description: Air France planes at the Charles de Gaulle airport (file photo).

item.title: Arsenal coach cooked a barbecue instead of watching title-deciding game
item.description: Mikel Arteta says he decided to not watch the match that secured Arsenal an historic EPL title, instead deciding to cook a barbecue.

media:group.media:description: Mikel Arteta said he could not watch the decisive game, so instead cooked a barbecue.

item.title: Former NT political staffer claims unfair dismissal, airs bullying allegations
item.description: A former staffer for Northern Territory government backbencher Clinton Howe is claiming she was sacked for refusing to falsify constituent information in a database, and was bullied and micromanaged by the politician's full-time electorate officer.
item.dc:creator: Jack Hislop

media:group.media:description: Clinton Howe's electoral office is located in the Gateway Shopping Centre in Palmerston.

item.title: Arsonists smash their way into Melbourne luxury car dealership
item.description: Arsonists struck a luxury car dealership in Melbourne's inner-east overnight, smashing into the building and setting it alight.

media:group.media:description: The dealership in Richmond following the attack.

item.title: What's going wrong inside the City of Perth?
item.description: It's been labelled 'dysfunctional' and 'diabolical'. What does the lord mayor have to say about the embattled City of Perth?
item.dc:creator: Courtney Withers and Gian De Poloni

media:group.media:description: Lord Mayor Bruce Reynolds is steering the City of Perth through turbulent times, but questions remain unanswered.

item.title: What you might not know about Pendlebury's lauded basketball background
item.description: Scott Pendlebury is about to set the record for most AFL matches played. This is the latest milestone in a stellar career that almost never was.
item.dc:creator: Bec Symons

media:group.media:description: Scott Pendlebury (right) was always a talented athlete.

item.title: Live: ASX to climb, oil drops on US–Iran deal hopes
item.description: Australian markets are set to rise this morning, as oil falls on hopes of a diplomatic way out of the war in Iran. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Adelaide Miller and Lin Lin

media:group.media:description: ASX Board. Bridge st Sydney

item.title: Live: Taylor accepts Nampijinpa Price's explanation for remarks on 2 Worlds Collide podcast
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor says senator Jacinta Nampijinpa Price's position on immigration is consistent with the Coalition's. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini

media:group.media:description: Angus Taylor holds a press conference on the morning of the Federal Budget.

item.title: Berlin approves bid to host Olympics on 100th anniversary of Nazi-run Games
item.description: Berlin's state parliament approves a bid to host the Olympics in 2036, on the 100-year anniversary of the Games hosted by Adolf Hitler and the Nazis.

media:group.media:description: American sprinter Jesse Owens speaks with German officials after claiming gold in the 200m at the 1936 Berlin Olympics.
